description: §65.2.4 `id × *` — an id selector (`#go {}`) and a class selector (`.btn {}`) both unconditionally set `color` and provably match one element (`<button id="go" class="btn">`). Under flat specificity the id carries NO extra weight (there is no `(id,class,type)` arithmetic, §65.5), so this is a same-level ambiguity → E-STYLE-CONFLICT (hard, error). This is the shape CSS would silently resolve id-beats-class; scrml refuses.
rationale: §65.2.4 lists `id × *` as a decidable shape (shared iff some element in the scope carries the intersection). §65.5: specificity is deleted — no id-beats-class tiebreak — so an id rule and a class rule that both match one element and set the same property are at the SAME precedence level → hard E-STYLE-CONFLICT.
